ISTANBUL SHAWARMA||430 COLLEGE ST M5T 1T.matchedAddress: 430 College St unit 1/2, Toronto, ON M5T 1T3, Canada
reviews:
18 combo plates ordered at 1:30 in the morning... a bit of a shock for a small place right...?
I called ahead and checked if they could do it.
They were a little hesitant that they had enough ingredients to make an order that large... in the end, they said yes and I placed the order.
It took a while (it's a small place remember...?) but the food was delivered in three large well-packed boxes.
It was piping hot and absolutely delicious !!
Not sure where to get halal schwarma...?
Look no further, this is it !!!

My friend and I went for dinner tonight. We decided to try shawarma for the first time. The place was very clean, and the staff was very attentive. The shawarma was delicious, and they even offered us some fries to try. I was really surprised by the great service! I’m happy to explore new places to eat in Canada. I highly recommend it; it’s a very clean place!

if istanbul shawarma was the only shawarma i could eat for the rest of my life, i would be a very happy person!

10/10 recommend the chicken shawarma on half fries half rice. amazing portions, crispy fries, and the most sauce used ever!

i have visited the past two days, and huge shoutout for staying open yesterday during the snowiest day of the year!!!

i will be back soon!

Update: I came back here and I changed my mind from 4 to 5 this time. They now accept credit card. The food is as good as my previous experience.
The food deserves 5*. Few concerns: first, a dirty washroom with no towels; second, not accepting credit card. Baklava was good.  Turkish was authentic. Shawerma Pide was something different than what I tried in Istanbul, but still it was good enough, especially the bread. The price is also affordable. Main food is on average less than 10$ and Tea is 2.5, Baklava the same. I come back here again if I revisit the district.

I had a plate with a mix of chicken and beef with salad. Both meats juicy and flavourful. Veggies were fresh. Baklava almost melted in my mouth. Outstanding food. Service was friendly and accommodating. Definitely recommended.
ISTANBUL SHAWARMA||430 COLLEGE ST M5T 1T.editorialSummary: Counter service Turkish small & shared plates in an easygoing setting with red interior details.

reviews: Really good poutine. I think they are known. As poutine lab on uber eats. Definitely recommended. My son Nico rated the poutine 10/10.

I ordered the vortex combo on uber eats.
The nashville chicken sandwich was mid, the chicken tender needs more seasoning. The chicken wings where not what I expected since its my first time buying wings from this location, the sauce for the wings where dried on unlike other places where the sauce would usually be visibly soaked after being tossed. The parmesean fries where okay. The meal was a 2 out of 5 for me, and to pay $31 after taxes and fees on uber eats it should be somewhat better.

After running errands I felt like wings for lunch, so I stopped in for some GarPar boneless wings. Service was swift, although expensive like $26 for a pound of wings, a can of diet coke, and upgraded the side to fire fries. If you love spicy like me, I suggest trying the fire fries. My mouth is still on fire an hour after finishing the fries. I rather enjoyed my experience, so I will be going back.

Do yourself a favour and don’t waste your money ordering from here. This place is on uber eats under the name Mac N Cheese Lab, but the address is the exact same as what’s listed here, and it was the biggest disappointment/scam ever.

First of all the pictures they use for each of the dishes is so damn misleading and nothing like the actual item you get. They had a buy one get one free deal for their chilli onion Mac for $14.99 And we decided to try that. I have attached a picture of how they advertise/describe it on ubereats versus what we actually got.

Not only was it disappointing, but it

I asked for the og tenders combo but they said they wouldn’t be able to make me fries, which I was confused about but decided I’d just get the tenders and the milkshake. Apparently a milkshake would take over 20 minutes to make which I was extremely confused by. I’m not sure if they were overly busy but I was the only customer inside. The tenders were good but I don’t understand how chicken tenders can take longer to make than a milkshake.

reviews: 7 of us cousin crew went to drupatis last night to eat food and shake our butts. The Dj was great, food was amazing. The jerk wings were fantastic. Pulowrie was so good, we liked the crispy one! And everything else we put in our mouths was fantastic. Drinks lovely. Carina our server was so so good to us, so pleasant and attentive. We definitely will be back. Thanks for lovely evening.

Experience was excellent no wait time excellent service
And our server carina very polite my second time there and it was beyond my expectations
Food was excellent

You want authentic good food that feels like your momma cooked it??? You come here portions are amazing service is top notch and the food will give you foodgasims thats how good it is fresh quality mmmm the doubles is loaded with goat the chow mein is out of the world and their chicken Wonton you will not be able to have just 1.
Everything was perfect, such an amazing atmosphere and the food feels like it was cooked with love! You have to come try and feel the vibes!

It was nice to try some 🇹🇹 favourites while visiting Toronto, and Drupatie’s offered a mix of highs and lows.

The dhal puri was okay, though the peas lacked the traditional depth of flavour and the texture leaned doughy on the edges, softish, but not silky. The potato was so overcooked it blended into the curry, and I was surprised there was no chana.

The pholourie was quite heavy, more like dumplings than the light, airy bites I expected, a real letdown.  The tamarind sauce was nice.

The doubles were decent: the bara was thin and nicely done, but the channa was far too salty for my taste.
THE ORIGINAL DRUPATIS||35 WOODBINE DOWNS.editorialSummary: Unassuming counter-serve joint offering large portions of Trinidadian-focused Caribbean fare.

reviews:
Let me first preface with a few minor things.
1/ The restaurant is VERY small. There's 3 tables so you might not have a place to sit. It can fit maybe 10 people at most in there. We were lucky that the 6 of us got a table.
2/ They do not speak any English, maybe 8 words. But the staff are super friendly even if it does get a little slow at times.
3/ Chili oil is not spicy at all. This is the only thing I would have to complain about.

Now let's get on with what's actually important. The food is some of the best and authentic Chinese cuisine I've ever had. Their steamed soup dumpling is AMAZING

Small family run restaurant. Specializing in dumplings, with frozen dumplings available as well. Food was excellent, best Xiao Long Bao I've had in GTA. Majority of dumpling options are steamed. Wontons in chili oil (+ Tahini) was also very enjoyable. Agree with another reviewer, the chili oil is not hot. People running the place were very welcoming and nice (very little/no English), and it felt like old school family restaurant with someone's little grandaughter hanging out on a Sunday evening :) Very much enjoyed eating here, will happily visit again. Not a huge menu (big enough) but we enjo

Another great kitchen specialized in buns and dumplings. It’s very tiny home kitchen kind of style with 3 tables inside only. We ordered:
1. Steamed pork buns 5/5
2. Steamed soup buns 5/5
3, pan fried dumplings 锅贴4/5
4. Seaweed 4/5
5. Shredded potatoes 5/5

Overall great quality of buns with reasonable price and good portion,especially compared to other similar places featuring buns ( mom’ pan fried buns , goubuli etc) however, this is a very small kitchen, they are more for takeouts

We went there for the first time today - the steamed buns and dumplings are amazing! Price point also super fair, meat portion more than most other similar places. Will definitely be back to try other dishes.

Very tasty xialong baos and really enjoyed the pan fried dumplings as well. The bean curds were on the salty side, but would pair well with rice. The sweet and sour sauce on the fried meat was delicious as well. The overall taste is homey but still have elevated flavors. The big baos were decent, but the other dishes we ordered shines a lot more. For the value, it was worth. Would recommend to others and come back again!

MIGENTE COCINA||823 DUNDAS ST W M6J 1V4.matchedAddress: 823 Dundas St W, Toronto, ON M6J 1V4, Canada
reviews:
I came here with a friend and we got the la casa meal and the seco de chivo and churros.

The la casa meal was okay. The grilled steak was more like stew beef and it was overly salty. The sausage pretty good. Veggie rice was okay. The best part was the pantacones for me.

The seco de chivo was okay. The goat stew was pretty good.

The churros were good but way too pricey for the size.

Just look at the horror story they served me!

When I called them about it to explain in a friendly manner about the “seco de pollo nightmare”, they were immediately passive aggressive and rude.

They even made up lies about the whole conversation!

The chicken and plantain could choke you to death.

The rice was 100% mush.

If I were the restaurant owner I’d be very concerned.

Do better, please!!!! 🙏

Got take out from this place tonight I am extremely disappointed. They took the Seco de Pollo literally and gave us the driest, blandest chicken I’ve ever tasted with overcooked rice. My husband can make a better Seco de Pollo and he’s only half ecuadorian. Their ceviche is made mainly of tomato and onion, and they were kind enough to put 3 tiny shrimps to actually get to call it ceviche. Will not be buying from this place again. Do not waste your money.

Started with the empanadas and nachos. Both great but the nachos (although the small size) could’ve had a bit more chips in it.

First time having Guaya fried rice and I was pleasantly surprised.

My wife had the sandwich and enjoyed it.

My mother in law had the Seco de Chivo and mentioned that there could’ve been more meat on the bone.

Overall, we enjoyed our visit.
Location was a bit hard to find and there’s limited street parking. There is a customer only washroom available.  Staff were great and the food was delicious.
Fantastic, authentic tacos. We had the beef, carne asada, chicharron, and fish. Spectacular flavours and great service!
MIGENTE COCINA||823 DUNDAS ST W M6J 1V4.query: MIGENTE COCINA 823 DUNDAS ST W Toronto

reviews:
We ordered the Kitoko (pondu -fufu) and Buga (egusi fufu) to try and the taste was exceptional! Will definitely be back to try more! The servers were so kind and accommodating.
Love African food and love how the restaurant celebrates Nigerian culture!

i am so happy we found this restaurant!
so amazing all around, i wish i could give 10 stars!
the server was so nice and asked us if we needed any explanations of the menus or any recommendations. it was very fast service and she was so funny and kind! great welcoming and friendly vibe
the fufu and egusi was so mouth watering flavourful. i could eat it 10 times over
the jollof rice and suya beef was seasoned so well! the flavours worked so well together and was so delicious.
perfect portions for two people to share two dishes
the ginger beer was delicious and so was the mango juice
good prices

I hosted a black history month dinner/games night and it was a success. The food, service, atmosphere and ambiance were amazing. My guests were well fed and loved the food. Thank you for hosting us, we will be back again.

First time here. Ordered the La Bassamoise (Afro-Vegan). It had a subtle flavor that was quite enjoyable, and the plantain was also tasty.

My main complaint is that none of the vegan dishes come with a protein option, which prevents the meal from being filling. I hope they'll consider adding protein sides to the vegan options in future.

It also took a bit long for our food to arrive.

Overall, the food was tasty and it was nice to try a different cultural cuisine than those I typically eat.
We celebrated a wonderful birthday dinner at Nganda! The food was delicious, service was impeccable and environment was lovely. Congrats to the owners!

NEPAL INDIA CURRY HOUSE||167 RONCESVALLE.matchedAddress: 167 Roncesvalles Ave, Toronto, ON M6R 2L3, Canada
reviews:
This is a really decent and affordable buffet in Roncesvalles, offering great value for the price. You’re not coming here for a premium fine-dining experience, but for cheap eats in Toronto that are filling, flavourful, and satisfying, it absolutely delivers.

There’s a solid variety of dishes to choose from. I’d recommend sticking with the butter chicken, chili chicken, Hakka fried rice, and vegetarian noodles — those were the standouts for me. Some of the dish names aren’t totally accurate (aside from the butter chicken and noodles), but honestly that’s not a big deal. The beauty of a buffet

Pretty good food overall, would come back to try the buffet special which is high value. During buffet hours you can still order off of the menu. We tried the chicken vindaloo, garlic naan, and steamed chicken Momos - naan was especially good. More of a hole in the wall vibe with good seating availability for small to moderately sized groups.

A simple restaurant with absolutely delicious food. I’ve tried many Indian places and can say Nepal India Curry house is by far the best I’ve tasted/had in a long long time!!

The food is flavourful yet not overpowering. We had the evening buffet - the variety (including meat options) for the price was amazing!! Service was great too. I could feel the food and service comes from the hearts of those who work here ❤️

We visited for the dinner buffet and asked about vegan options. While the selection wasn’t extensive, there was more than enough for us to enjoy. The staff were incredibly kind and attentive and even prepared a few extra vegan dishes for us complimentary, which we really appreciated.
The food tasted great and, unlike many Indian or Nepali places that tend to go heavy on unnecessary spices, everything here felt well balanced. We felt great after the meal, which says a lot.
Delicious food, thoughtful service, and we’ll definitely be visiting again.

Best value in town.

Not many places in Toronto where you can get such a high quality selection of Indian/Nepali food for the prices they're offering right now!

14.99 for lunch and 17.99 for dinner with made too order Naan and spectacular Butter Chicken + Goat Curry and many other dishes at a variety of spice levels.

Everything from Pekora starters to Ice Cream and Gulab Jamun to finish.

It brings me all the way back to more than a decade ago when you could find such great value downtown.

If you're in the area and enjoy this type of cuisine at all. You're making a horrible mistake not comi

reviews: Their food Took me back to Amritsar.

Had an amazing experience at Doaba Junction! The food was fresh, flavorful, and full of authentic Punjabi taste. The service was friendly, and the atmosphere felt warm and welcoming. Every dish was packed with rich spices and homemade-style quality. Definitely a great spot for anyone craving delicious Indian food. Highly recommend!
Amazing food! Hakka noodles, Amritsari naan, and chana were all spot on. Paneer gravy dishes are next level. Great service too. Highly recommend!

One of my friends recommended this place and I am glad I tried. This is a take out and caterers limited seating area but a good experience. Tandoori Soya Chaap and Malai Soya Chaap is really nice. We also tried Amritsari Kulcha and it was also delicious.

Parking available at the front and service is good.

Absolutely amazing butter chicken at Dobha Junction Indian Kitchen! The chicken was tender, the gravy was rich, creamy, and full of authentic flavor. Perfect balance of spices with fresh naan on the side made the meal even better. Great service and cozy atmosphere too. Definitely one of the best butter chicken spots I’ve tried. Highly recommend!

reviews:
Saw this in Etobicoke and got super excited, always in search of a decent chai spot. Though it was late, it was busy with only one staff working. We didn’t mind the wait, but did see others frustrated.

Opted to try the regular chai & omelette.

The chai was average, to be honest I didn’t even finish it or drink  half of it. I wanted to enjoy it so badly, as it’s close to home but it didn’t live up to any expectations.

The omelette was alright!

Overall, that excitement was short lived. However, they have several items to try from including dessert so there is something for everyone. The pric

A truly delightful spot that never disappoints! This charming little café offers an impressive variety of flavourful snacks that pair beautifully with their aromatic chai. Every item is prepared with care, bursting with authentic taste and freshness. The cozy ambience adds to its charm — warm, inviting, and perfect for a quick bite or a relaxing tea break. The staff are exceptionally courteous and always make the experience pleasant. Though the place is small, it has an unmistakable charm that makes you want to return again and again. A hidden gem for anyone who appreciates good chai and vibra

I ordered Afghan Paneer sandwich, and this is what I was given. The cheese was cold, not even melted properly and it has lettuce and bell peppers. Last time it was completely different. There is no sauce in the sandwich. No marination of Afghani sauce in the Paneer looks very dry and sandwich was totally cold. Service was super slow. I called and mention that last time and which was different, and they said this is our standard recipe. I visited to this store multiple times. It is on a good location, but the thing is staff is least interested in working. I don’t know whenever I’ve been there,

Lovely first visit to Chai Stop, Etobicoke!
We tried Chai Stop for the first time and were really impressed. The owner clearly has a great vision for this place — it’s warm, welcoming, and has a great vibe. We tried their tea and filter coffee with cookies — both were absolutely worth trying! The vada pav was delicious. The staff were friendly and attentive, making the experience even better.

Definitely a nice spot to relax, enjoy good chai, and grab a quick bite.

The chai was absolutely wonderful freshly made and full of flavor. The samosa is one of the best I’ve had, super crisp and tasty. Tried a few of their cookies too and they were fresh and crunchy. The owner is really friendly and the place has such good vibes. Definitely worth a visit!

reviews:
I walk by this place every single day, and today, on my way to the grocery store, I realized I didn't actually want to deal with groceries. I’ve always noticed the lunch special at Umi’s, and I’m so glad I finally decided to stop in.

Places like this are truly the heart of a community. In a world of big chains, we should be intentionally supporting these local gems that keep the neighborhood feeling like home. The service was fast and friendly, but more importantly, the food was excellent. I had the stew chicken, coleslaw, and rice and peas, and it was delicious comforting, authentic, and exa

I finally was able to attend UMI's Kitchen after so many attempts by UBER and DoorDash as it always says unavailable. I was in the neighborhood and decided to stop by. I thought it was located more east on Dundas but it's actually near Dundas and Sherbourne. Toronto has tried to revive the neighborhood but, it has the same energy. I was lucky as parking is street parking so expect to park and run in to get your order. It looked closed when I got out and the door is not new but, I got in. It isn't a big place but the lady who got my order was pleasant. I ordered a small Oxtail (not cheap no mor

FYI the photos DO NOT reflect the current situation of the restaurant, completely runned down with flies all over the food counter but despite all that out of respect and wanting to promote a family owned business I proceeded to order. I ordered and paid for a large jerk chicken. I received 3 scoops or rice and a quarter chicken with a tiny piece despite being served with no coleslaw, which I asked for. The business partner out of "concern" asks if im given less chicken. I responded YES but nothing was done about it. Also, the large plate as per the menu is 12.99 but I was billed 15.81 !!. Suc

Homey space in the downtown area, so keep in mind of the possible population around the shop. Nevertheless the oxtails had a good flavor just fatty and not enough meat for me, but the rice and beans were good especially with the gravy. Jerk was good seasoned well 1/1 white and dark with rice and beans. Give a try if you’re by

Im writing this as I eat. Don’t be fooled by the neighbour hood. Scoops of rice with beans and three pieces of BBQ chicken for $5 is a great deal. It is very tasty and filling (if you’re a huge eater spend $10 on two orders). Service was nice and will coming back again.

We tried all 3 kinds of Momo's (veggie, chicken and buffalo) and their chai and samosa and everything was so fresh and authentic and delicious and we absolutely loved it. Definitely a hidden gem and a great go to spot for authentic Nepalese cuisine. Thank you!!!

Northern Taste has amazingly superior authentic Nepali taste. Their chicken momo and buff momo is so good, I have already became their regular customer. I recommend this place for anyone who loves Momo and more Nepali cuisine.

We had tried both Momo and Thukpa at Northern Taste. The Momo was really good, mouth watering!  Highly recommend this place to all Momo lovers.
Worth a repeat visit!

WING STATION||2901 MARKHAM RD, #4 M1X 0B.matchedAddress: 2901 Markham Rd #4, Scarborough, ON M1X 0B7, Canada
reviews: Wing station is my family’s favourite to-go-place in Scarborough when craving for wings & burger. Wings & fries is always a good combo but for us, their house rice (Pat’s rice) is the best combo for the wings. It is not available all the time. What we do is to call ahead while making our way to the restaurant and order ahead the Pat’s rice. Their service is outstanding. Order normally takes 25-35 mins so plan accordingly. This place is a gem for us with great food, value for money and amazing service!

My second visit here.

FOOD
Food was heavily seasoned which made it 10/10. We got the TWIN SPECIAL = great deal for couples. Came with two flavours, 2 drinks, and chili onion fries. Also, the wings were not tiny as you would typically get at other wing spots. They were medium sized. We had extra to take home.

The mozzarella cheese sticks was definitely not worth $9 though…

PARKING
Located in a plaza = lots of parking space.

STAFF
Friendly. Cant complain.

Super clean place, amazing service and the food is bomb! Highly recommend you try the chili fries but heads up it's spicy. For wings, found level 4 was comfortable spicy, but next time we will try level 5-6 and maybe bring some friends :)

I recently visited Wing Station in Scarborough and had a great experience! The wings were cooked perfectly—crispy on the outside, juicy inside, and full of flavor. They have a wide variety of sauces to choose from, and the staff was really friendly and quick with service. The portions were generous for the price, and the place had a nice, casual vibe. Definitely a go-to spot if you’re craving good wings in Scarborough

Wing Station is found in one of those sketchy plaza. My friends tell me that they have some really good wings flavors found here.

We ordered the 1.5 lb wings, and the 5 flavored wings platter. So, in total, 6 flavors of wings. The flavors that we got are Chili and  Onion (x7 spicy level, dry rub), Sour Cream and Onion (dry rub), Garlic Explosion (dry rub), Korean (sauce), Italian (sauce), and Creamy Cajun (sauce). We also ordered the Bacon And Cheddar Fries. And got some cups of tap water.

The menu has appetizers, burgers, wraps, over 100+ wing flavors, desserts etc.they have many different

reviews: I used to live in England many years ago, and Pret A Manger was always my favourite place for breakfast and coffee. I was really happy to see it here in Canada. The place looks new, very clean, and the customer service is polite and friendly. It honestly reminds me of the UK. If you ever miss England, come here and grab a coffee. I highly recommend it.

This place is so reminiscent of my student days in London, UK : it feels like my old canteen for a quick hot wrap or soup. I’m really glad they’ve opened a new location in the PATH, which makes it super convenient. We tried the tomato soup and the chicken hot wrap, and both tasted exactly like how I remembered them. The cinnamon roll and almond croissant were also very nice. I’ll definitely be coming back regularly.

Love Pret, was happy this location opened up as I live nearby. No issue with food, I believe it was the manager of the store who didn’t behave favourably towards me during my visit. I changed to sweats and a hoodie after work and decided to pay a visit. Maybe she didn’t show me courtesy or respect as I wasn’t dressed properly. She was very arrogant and rude when I asked a question about the food. Been going to Pret in the UK for years before it came to Canada, this was my first bad experience, and that too because of the service.
Absolutely fantastic service!
Tasty and healthy meals

reviews:
This is such a beautiful place that needs more customers. Or maybe it's because I sat down here during a slow period, but I loved how chill and cozy this place is.

Had the Cheese and Chutney Toastie and the Karak Chaii 50/50. The toastie was nice and light, and the chaii was just right. I might try it with no sugar next time, but the 50/50 was not crazy sweet, which is my preference.

I hope more people find this place. Their prices are very reasonable. Spent just under $10 for these two items.

I have been here twice so far. It's a cool chill spot 😎 I tried different dishes on each visit. First time I had Aloo sandwiche and 2nd time a breakfast dish. Both dishes were yummy. I had Chai Latte with oat milk as well.

Visited Chaiiwallah of London in Markham and immediately knew something was off when they offer incentives for a review.
The chai, was actually good.
But then came the butter chicken paratha… and a bonus ingredient I definitely didn’t order: a hair. That pretty much sent my appetite packing.
Also, every topping is extra: onion 50 cents, tomato 50 cents, cheese 50 cents. At this rate, I’m glad the paratha itself isn’t sold separately and assembled like IKEA furniture.
Tea: great. Everything else: character-building.

Cozy little cafe! Clean and the staff was so friendly. I got the butter chicken poutine and the soda of the day was flavoured with saffron. Highly recommend for a sit down meal or grabbing some tasty drinks to go and get your day started!

I come here often with my wife and we absolutely love it! The karak chai is amazing — it even reminds my wife of the chai back home in India. The food is always delicious and the customer service is excellent. Highly recommend Chaiiwala to anyone looking for authentic taste and a great experience!

This is some of the best sushi I've ever had. I loved the flavor of the sushi; I ordered different flavors, the food was fresh, and the service was excellent.

reviews: Amazing spot in the upper beaches community. Kerem (owner) is a great host and they have incredible food, much of which has been inspired by his home country of Turkey. In addition to the fresh pasta and baked goods, we just tried the breakfast and it was incredible. Light, healthy and tasty. Highly recommend for their coffee, baked treats (simit!) and the pasta of course.

Kerem and his staff are warm and welcoming to all. The vibe is very friendly and relaxing for a cup of Turkish coffee or tea and baklava or a pasta delight with Turkish touch.
I had a lovely chat with Kerem about his connection with the local community.
He even attended a beloved Nona Maria's funeral to the surprise of her family.
I had Turkish tea with carrot baklava. Bought Turkish delight for a gift and lentil soup for dinner. Yum!

Wow! This is now a favourite place of mine to eat.

The pastas are delicious, I need to come back to try more! Better pastas than I’ve had at expensive Italian restaurants.
The pasta is made fresh every day.

On top of delicious food, a super friendly owner Kerem delivers top service.

Decor is super cute. Highly recommend, you must try out this place!!!

Went today with my father for lunch. The pasta is very special, super fresh and tasty. I'm vegetarian and there were plenty of options, I had the mediterranean pasta and really liked it. My dad had a dish with meat dumplings, also enjoyed it. We felt super welcomed, absolutely outstanding service. Looking forward to go again!

Fantastic pasta spot! We spotted this beauty on our way home one day and noted we had to try it. Showed up a week ago and ordered the smoked eggplant pasta, it was so delicious that we got the nonna's bolognese to go. Had it the next day and it was just as good as day one.

The pasta is somehow light and the flavours are rich. I believe the owner said the ingredients are imported from Turkia and Italy. I loved that there's no feeling of bloating or heaviness after eating.

I also love that this place is halal. I feel like we don't have many halal based pasta spots, so I'm happy I was able to g

Amazing food and quick service! Stopped by for lunch and ordered the Korean BBQ chicken shawarma bowl. Generous portion size and delicious meal. Staff were super friendly and the place is very clean! There’s a few seats to sit and eat-in. Highly recommend!

Had an amazing experience at Tahini last night! The food was absolutely delicious super fresh and full of flavor. I got my order within just 2 minutes no waiting at all.The service was excellent too the staff were really friendly and helpful. It was a bit loud inside but everything else more than made up for it. One of the staff members even asked me about my preferred toppings which was a nice personal touch. Definitely recommend this place!

Had a great lunch here, food was extremely fresh and the staff were all very welcoming. I had the Habanero Chicken Wrap, the chicken was juicy and the wrap was some of the best bread I’ve had in a long time. The chicken shawarma rice bowl is my favourite from Tahini’s. This location is new and is always clean, the wait time isn’t long. Highly recommended for shwarma lovers!

MEXI-PAK FUSION TACO'S||417 SPADINA AVE.matchedAddress: 417 Spadina Ave., Toronto, ON M5T 2G6, Canada
reviews:
Wonderful marriage of Mexican and Pakistani flavours. I have always wanted this fusion because they truly are meant for each other and I am extremely happy to know it has been done.

I was weary at first because it is something that is easy to mess up or not go far enough with, but to my pleasant surprise it was just the right amount of fusion. It blended foundation Pakistani and Mexican flavours with finesse — biryani and cheese as a base foundational and protein to pair — into a taco I would be proud to serve myself. I can’t even imagine the amount of labour that goes into each taco. birria

I happened to discover this place by accident when I casually glanced to my left while walking, and I'm so glad I did! It's everything I love about both cuisines put together. I had the beef kabob taco and it tasted sooo good, it had rice and peas and onion and meat etc all in a crispy-yet-nicely-chewy shell. The food tasted fluffy and home-made, and not overly oily or overly spicy. The chutney/salsa smelled fresh.

The dessert was also great. The Mexican flan was soft and not too sweet, and the kheer tasted like the kind of kheer you get at desi weddings.

Overall I'm still dreaming about tha
I’ve been saying Mexican and Pakistani food would go so well together as a Pakistani myself. And wow was I proven right. What a beautiful blend of cuisines. Thank you so much for the amazing food
We loved it and will be back sooon!! Great prices, amazing food, great service. Love the decor. The Okra tacos and chicken tikka tacos were so yummy.

Delicious homemade torta and tacos!! Wonderful and friendly owners,  you can really tell the food is made with love and passion to bring you the best exceptional dishes. My little pit stop before home now, finally a fusion taco spot in the neighborhood! A must try if your around no doubt, I ate the torta and had a jarrito to wash it down. The price is right as well can’t go wrong

I really liked their croissants for their taste and value. Their almond croissant was flakely and generously filled with almond paste. I also tried their cookie, but thought it was okay.

Would recommend and come back.

My favorite croissant in the city. Marc is a genius and I'm very happy to have him in our neighborhood. The eclair is the best I've had in my life. His chou à la crème also, amazing. The breads,  outstanding.  Merci!

Heard that this coffee spot moved from down the street. Very beautiful interior with some seating and also sale of dried goodies such as pasta sauces and noodles.

They have some amazing croissants that are perfectly crispy and soft and buttery on the inside. The cookie was a tad too sweet but was baked very well.

Great music. Food was awesome. The chicken and Brie was a great option. We ate in and had a nice time. Highly recommend that you buy a sourdough loaf. Wow. That bread was so good for dinner and breakfast the next day. Perfection. Next time, I am definitely eating some croissant or donuts!!!!!

Walking by and caught this little gem that just opened in hood. Outstanding breakfast sammie on a croissant. The atmosphere is spot on. I wanted to hang out all morning but I had to get to work... Shoulda taken a moment to enjoy the sandwich in the cafe.

Ordered late night takeout from here it was finger licking good they deserve 6 stars. Yuvraj is an excellent person amazing conversation just love this place coming back again. 100% recommended.

reviews: Tried three matcha drinks along with the earl grey cream puff, cereal milk basque cheesecake, and brown butter confetti brownie. The strawberry matcha was excellent and the pastries stood out for being flavorful without being too sweet!
My favorite bakery & cafe in toronto!!!!! Will be going back

usually not a fan of matcha but the cashier spoke highly of the nitro matcha and she was right !! loved the strawberry matcha !! so smooth and you can tell they use real strawberries and high quality matcha

the egg tarts with the croissant shell and the strawberry chiffon cake are to die for !!

loved the service and aesthetic ! def bringing my friends for a yap session soon
Definitely my new favorite bakery!!! The vibes were top tier and everything was delicious ( and I’m usually super picky lol )

So excited to have a laptop-free spot to just relax and chat with friends ✨ I made the mistake of ordering a drink that was far too acidic for me and they really kindly accommodated and made me a strawberry matcha that was phenomenal! 🥹 the apple cinnamon bun was yum and after a couple hours of chatting with a friend they gave us some cookies to try which were delish! 💕 can’t wait to see Maison 118 thrive

CHOP CHOP GOOD FOOD FAST||771 DUNDAS ST.matchedAddress: 771 Dundas St W, Toronto, ON M6J 1T9, Canada
reviews: Every dish we ordered was amazing: the mapu tofu, ginger beef, dumplings and Chinese Broccoli. The family who own the spot is also adorable. I will definitely keep coming back.

Yesterday, I was having a really great noodle soup from this restaurant. Simple but really delicious. The soup itself is firing, Taiwanese mixed with Cantonese style, where authenticity and creativity meet together. As a person who travelled across Canada to Asia, this is definitely the best soup noodle I have ever have. And the owners are really nice and warming, absolutely the ceiling of a family style restaurant. If you are looking for incredible Asian cuisine, this would be a must-go place.

Small shop family is all involved.  All very friendly and polite.
The food was brilliant.  I had the baby bok choy with BBQ pork, steamed rice and cucumber salad. The cucumber salad was beyond delicious. Fresh crispy cucumber in a spicy vinegar dressing that I could eat everyday.  The bok chop was flavourful, cooked to perfection and succulent. The rice was well rice tasty, cooked just right and needed nothing. The pork was, to me, the star of the show. Cooked perfectly with a lovely BBQ sauce that was neither overly sweet or spicy.
I will be back. And I will bring others .

Went here with a friend and had a nice overall experience. The owner was really kind, and it was honestly sweet seeing two little girls there (I think they were his daughters). The older one was feeding the younger one and chatting with her, and the little one was sitting on her sister — super wholesome.

Went on a rainy cold day a few weeks back. Portions were generous, food was very well priced, and service was great.

You can tell the food is made with love and care. And it comes piping hot. Come here for great food.

OMAI RICE BAR||21 BALDWIN ST M5T 1L1.matchedAddress: 21 Baldwin St, Toronto, ON M5T 1L1, Canada
reviews: Came here for lunch on a Thursday afternoon. The person behind the counter was quick to take my order and prepare the food. Waited about 8 mins for them to prepare the bowl. It was really good and the presentation was great. Will definitely come back soon.

At first glance, a very unassuming rice bar. And then an impeccably plated bowl of their miso glazed salmon got in front of me. Beautifully fresh ingredients, distinct flavours coming together in great harmony, not competing against each other. Service was lovely too, the person behind the counter truly cares about assembling each dish. And portion size is perfect! Came in for "a quick dinner before an event" and ended up discovering a gem that I will be definitely returning to. Thank you very much!

decided to stop by this place while i waited for a friend and wow what a good decision it was! i got the korean fried chicken rice bowl and it was soooooo good. perfect amount of sweet, spicy, sour, savory. the chicken had a super nice crunch and the dressing on the salad was addicting. and all for under $20. you can’t go wrong. i’ll definitely be back! and also everyone working there was so nice!

Fine Dining in a Bowl, Fast

So for the same price of Manchu Wok or really any fast food combo, I can get clean, fresh, quality food from classically trained chefs (from their sister restaurant Omai).

Omai Rice Bar nails what so many spots try and fail to do: deliver elevated, restaurant-quality food with the speed and convenience of fast food. It’s the kind of place that makes you pause mid-bite and think, how is this not being served at every fast casual establishment.

TIP: ask for their hot sauce

Such a cute place — menu is simple and delicious. I highly recommend the Crispy Pork Belly bowl, perfect level of spice with the gochujang. Will definitely be coming back! Just to note, there isn’t very much seating… it’s more of a takeout place.

Top notch Jamaican restaurant with fresh food. Rice and peas are a perfect cook, chef gave me half and half but the jerk takes the crown here. Fried chicken still great. Shout out to the chef doing his thing in that kitchen - there are tons of Jamaican spots in the GTA but this food is easy 10/10

The curry goat roti is truly mouth-watering and incredibly tasty. The goat is tender and perfectly seasoned, wrapped in soft, fresh roti that soaks up all that flavorful curry sauce. It’s the kind of dish you crave long after you’ve finished it.

reviews: The nachos were the best thing we had 🤩🤩🤩 absolutely delicious tortilla chips and lots of toppings!! Guacamole was also really fresh. It is honestly hard to fail at preparing completos, yet somehow they managed to do so by adding sauces and ingredients far beyond the traditional recipe. What should be a simple, balanced, and iconic Chilean dish became something unrecognizable.

The empanadas were a sad shadow of what they are in Chile. I had never even heard of chorizo empanadas before—an unfortunate mutation that you can try here if you’re curious. They lacked the flavor, texture, and authenticity that define a proper Chilean empanada.

Overall, the experience felt more like a form of cultural appropriation than a respectfu

Our server recommended the classic churrasco and the tacos - we got both - and it was very good. The meat (sliced beef and sliced pork) were cooked on a plancha and had a tasty charred flavour to it. There was a tad too much sauce in the sandwich for my personal liking, but I appreciated that the condiments were served cold and cut the richness of the meat.

The pisco sour here is authentic and tastes amazing. I liked the chicken empanadas better, as the beef was a bit too oily. Service could be better. Regardless we had a great time and really enjoyed the ambience.

reviews:
⭐️⭐️⭐️ 3/5

Good Variety, Clean Space — But Service & Consistency Need Improvement

Dosa Boyz is a pure vegetarian South Indian kitchen offering an extensive dosa menu with creative combinations. We ordered the Garlic Cheese Masala Dosa, Medu Vada, Nilgiri Green Dosa, Schezwan Spring Dosa, Mysore Masala Dosa, and Paneer Bhurji Dosa, giving us a solid variety to sample.

Food Quality:
The dosas were decent, but portion sizes and stuffing were noticeably lacking, especially considering the pricing. The sambar was okay, while the chutneys were unfortunately watered down and bland, which took away

Absolutely loved the food here! I had paper dosa, Medu Vada with three chutneys and sambar, along with a masala tea — everything was delicious and fresh. So happy to find a place that’s 100% pure veg.

The front desk staff were very kind and welcoming, which made the experience even better.

Tip: Don’t forget to add ghee to the paper dosa — I missed it this time, but definitely trying that next visit! 😊

First welcomed with very nice incense smell reminding of home opening up all senses. Nandita was awesome server. I cherished and appreciate her hospitality.
The food the presentation, the authenticity of taste of food was surprising. I would never go anywhere else for filter coffee dosa and idli . Home away from home .

I tried Masala Chai, Idli Sambhar combo and Mysore Masala Dosa. All of them were so delicious and tasty. All three Chutney's were so fresh and good. Service by Brijal was great. She is so polite , helpful, suggestive and friendly. I wouldn recommend to try everything. They are also offering unlimited free refill of Chutney and Sambhar. Best in town !
Thank you for your Service, will visit again soon 😊.

(Tried the butter ghee dosa and the Hakka style veg noodles). Great food. Fair prices. Great ambience. Dosas had great packing too and doesn’t lose its crispness even after an hour. Exceptional service. Highly recommended (particularly for the dosas).

reviews:
After your Costco run at Leaside, Chachee’s cafe is best place to relax for a good cup of Chai, Chaat and Sandwiches.

I loved their Pistachio tres leches and dirty pink chai. Aaloo tiki could have been crisp in chaat.

This cafe truly raises the bar when it comes to variety and quality. Whether you're in the mood for a hearty breakfast, a quick lunch, or something sweet to go with your coffee, the menu has something for everyone—without sacrificing flavor or freshness.

What stood out to me most was the thoughtful balance of classic comfort dishes and creative, modern twists. There’s a genuine sense that the kitchen team cares about what they’re putting on each plate.

Pair all that with a cozy atmosphere and warm, attentive service, and you've got a spot that's easy to return to—again and again.
The service here was amazing, the man who took our order was kind and patient and served us with a smile :)) love chachees!

Chachee’s Chai Café never disappoints when it comes to food and chai. The snacks are fresh, flavorful, and perfectly paired with their amazing chai, making every visit feel satisfying and comforting. The menu offers great variety, and everything is well-prepared with authentic taste and quality ingredients. It’s a perfect spot for light meals, evening cravings, or catching up with friends in a cozy atmosphere.

Shreya provided excellent customer service and made the experience even more pleasant.

Highly recommended for great food, chai, and overall experience!

New favourite café unlocked! Their Kashmiri tea should be illegal—it's that addictive. The poutine was pure happiness on a plate, and the almond croissant? Gone in 30 seconds. The vibe is great, the staff is super friendly, and the menu is like a desi street food + burger party. If you don’t go, you’re missing out on joy. Highly recommend!

Amazing Persian cuisine recently has been opened in downtown. We tried 3 different foods and I cannot choose between them, super duper delicious. However, if I one to recommend one, I would say “Kebab Beef Torsh” is a must try. We diffidently gonna come back again.

I travel from far and I happen to find this place after hours of trying to find a decent place to dine in without reservation on valentines…
this place not only takes you to Iran with its delicious cuisine but it is genuinely the best food I’ve ever had please do support this business they’ve been open just under a year. The portions are MASSIVE BTW. Very warm and welcoming people thank you for making me and my mother’s night.❤️

Very solid kebab here. Ordered the vaziri kebab platter and it was easily enough for two people. Extremely generous portions. They also provided some sauces to try alongside the kebab. I didn't feel like these were necessary but it was a nice touch.

reviews:
A hidden gem everyone should know about!
I must say this place is a hidden spot that more people need to discover. The food was absolutely amazing. I tried the paper dosa and the ghee dosa — both were outstanding, crispy, flavorful, and perfectly made. Highly recommended!

The restaurant has a large dining space, including a comfortable family dining area. Everything was clean, and the service was excellent — friendly, attentive, and quick. Overall, a wonderful dining experience and definitely worth visiting again.

Ate dinner here with a friend. Ordered the Mini Idly, Spinach Masala Dosa, and Paneer Dosa. Everything tasted great! And the sambar, chutney, and all the other dips were top notch. I do prefer the Paneer Dosa, but spinach is good too!

This is the 3rd time I've been to this restaurant and they do not disappoint.

For the items ordered with tax and tip come to just under $50. So, $25 per person.

We ordered Mysore Masala Dosa , Podi Idli and Medu Vada

Dosa had so much garlic , I couldn’t taste other flavours

Podi Idli was delicious but then the powder had some hard ingredients it was so tiny that was getting stuck and making it uncomfortable to eat. So it was spoiling the mood.

Vada was sad !! Not at all crispy or hot .. disappointed

They served so little chutney and they charge extra for chutney. There are so many South Indian restaurants in Scarborough that serves good food and don’t charge for Chutney absolutely. How will one not take extra chutney with huge dosa or such dry Vad

Waitress was super diligent, kind  and informative. She was amazing and made us feel welcomed. Probably the best service we have received in years.  Food unfortunately was bland, but presentation was beautiful.

reviews:

The tiramisu iced latte was absolutely delicious — creamy, flavorful, and perfectly balanced. Definitely the standout drink for me! ☕🤎

The desserts tasted really good as well. I’m not a big fan of the cappuccino there compared to other cafés, but overall it was still a pleasant experience.

From the moment I walked into Kanafani Sweets & Coffee, I knew I’d found something special. The atmosphere is stylish yet cozy, with inviting decor and a welcoming vibe that instantly makes you feel at ease.

But what truly stole the show for me was their kunafa. They don’t simply keep it sitting around—it’s made fresh, on the spot, the moment you order. You can taste the difference: crisp, hot, perfectly balanced sweetness, and that unforgettable Middle Eastern texture and flavor. If you love authentic desserts, this alone is worth the visit.

Every staff member I encountered was friendly, at

Newest addition to the cafe scene in Scarborough, located in the Lawrence and Warden plaza.

They're open late, which is a plus since most cafes in the area close by 5. The ambiance is decent with plenty of seating, and the place is almost always busy!

When I say they have the city's best tiramisu latte, I'm not kidding. Their iced tiramisu latte is honestly one of the best in town. I'd highly recommend trying it.

The strawberry cups are also well done, with a generous pistachio crunch and fresh strawberries dipped in chocolate and marshmallow.

Need to try their matcha next time I’m there.

Food - amazing Palestinian themed desserts, prepared fresh and in house. Heavenly choices for those with a sweet tooth.
Service - wonderful staff, warm, friendly, polite and professional. I felt welcomed and respected
Atmosphere- exceptionally well done, very well balanced and plain and elegant. Music volume is appropriate, seating is plentiful and the walls are beautifully decorated with images of Palestine and its people.
Definitely recommended for a cost effective and smoke-free hangout spot, and it’s worth commuting for

I have tried all the middle eastern coffee cafes in the GTA and this one is hands down my favorite and nothing can beat it. We’ve come here countless times and everytime the coffee and desserts have never failed! Literally hubs and I favorite place to go for any occasion, thank god they opened in Scarborough because we used go endlessly to the Mississauga location! Go to favs are tiramisu lattes & cake and chocolate berry cups!
KANAFANI SWEETS & COFFEE||2082 LAWRENCE.query: KANAFANI SWEETS & COFFEE 2082 LAWRENCE AVE E, B Toronto

DUHOK SHAWARMA||1635 LAWRENCE AVE W, #10.matchedAddress: 1635 Lawrence Ave W, North York, ON M6L 3C9, Canada
reviews: Wow! This place is great. The beef plate is super tasty, especially the rice. You can tell the beef is high quality because the fat makes the whole plate taste amazing. Really nice vibe inside, too. I'll definitely be back…!

This couple seriously knows what they’re doing. This isn’t your run of the mill shawarma restaurant. The meats are seasoned marvellously bursting with flavour, the rice is cooked perfectly, the veggies are all fresh. Absolutely delicious, new fav spot. I also tried a side of lentil soup, very clearly home made with care, tasty and warmed my soul.
The new owners (as of mid 2025) are so kind and they’ve clearly got high standards. The flavour and quality of the meat speaks for itself. Must try

Excellent food and great service. The falafels are made fresh to order (crisp on the outside, soft on the inside). Both the kabob and chicken souvlaki plates were amazing…beautifully seasoned and generous portions. Highly recommend!

Order a kabab party platter instead of your typical boring pizza! The Best Assyrian/Iraqi/Kurdistani/Turkish owned Shawarma/Kebabs in town! Bkalava and other sweets are available with nice Middle Eastern tea. Catering available. Excellent attentive service! I would definitely come back and their prices are excellent as well!

I would like to share my experience. I have been coming to this location since 2018 (since Red and White) and have enjoyed most of my visits.

After a few ownership/management changes, I can confidently say that Duhok Shawarma once again made me feel like I was welcomed and eating at someone’s home again.

They are gracious, friendly, and accommodating. I had the mixed shawarma pide (give it a try) and mixed shawarma plate. Everything was prepared fresh and portions are great.

The “dry” jerky pieces that one reviewer mentioned are because the outside of the meats are purposefully broiled. It
DUHOK SHAWARMA||1635 LAWRENCE AVE W, #10.query: DUHOK SHAWARMA 1635 LAWRENCE AVE W, #10 Toronto

DECCAN HOUSE RESTAURANT||1885 KENNEDY RD.matchedAddress: 1885 Kennedy Rd, Scarborough, ON M1P 2L9, Canada
reviews:
This is one gem in Scarborough takes the cup when it comes to biriyani in Canada! It's best biriyani you could ever get! You get the option to choose single or double masala for biriyani and nothing screams authentic more than that question.

The food is straight from Hyderabad, there's nothing in Canada that could get close to Hyderabadi than Deccan! The Haleem is a must try, and you can tell by the consistency and the way it smells!

Best rates I've even seen in Canada and authentic as it could get. Deccan House team you've done such a fabulous work that you've created something really speci

The food was so, so, so good it  reminded us of back home. Every bite was full of authentic flavors. The biryani was absolutely delicious and tasted just like home.
What made the experience even better was the service. The owner personally came and spoke with us, which made us feel very welcomed and valued. The staff were extremely friendly, attentive. Hands down, one of the best service experiences we’ve had. Highly recommended for anyone craving authentic taste and heartfelt hospitality.

Finally an authentic Hyderabadi restaurant. Food which tastes like the original with no excuses. Great ambience with all the murals looking fine from the walls from Tipu Sultan to the Charminar. Looking at good service and good food. The Mughlai Goat curry was rich and well cooked and the Hyderabadi biryani just right. Go enjoy good cuisine.

A really great experience. The food and service was top tier. Loved everything we tried. The Curries, Dum Biryanis, Special Mutton Haleem, and the Tea was 10/10. The only thing better was their service. Especially one of the employees, Utpreksha, was very nice and extremely knowledgeable about their menu and suggested the best items for everyone. Would definitely like to go again.

I’ve been here twice and everything is delicious! Had the paneer and mutton fun biryani, goat soup and Gobi 65. Male server made excellent recommendations. I just wish there was more gravy or meat in the biryani. Is mostly rice which is biryani but for an entree I expect more protein. Otherwise yummy ❣️😀🕊️
DECCAN HOUSE RESTAURANT||1885 KENNEDY RD.query: DECCAN HOUSE RESTAURANT 1885 KENNEDY RD Toronto

reviews: I was really excited for this place. But it was the biggest bamboozle false advertising ever … the nan is not nan literally you don’t want nan of the food it’s so hard I could chip a tooth and soooooo dry. It’s was literally a rock….. the pictures are very deceiving because I got a calzone looking thing instead of nan. Also the picture of the chia in is in a clear glass which naturally indicates it’s a cold beverage. If it only comes hot it should be displayed in a mug. The chia was ok. But the food is a big no. See photo for reference and compare to the menu photo literally not the same item.

Amazing stuff naan. I tried vegetarian option chaap. Test amazing.
Must try badam milk.

I was so excited to see a samosa shop near work that I bought 6 chicken and 6 potato samosas to take home for the family.
We were so disappointed by the samosas!  The chicken samosa was soooo dry that meat crumbs completely fell out of the dough. This was the first time I was given sweet Thai chili and ketchup for samosas.  Never again!
The stuffed chicken naan is sooo gooddd! Their samosas are tasty too. :)  Friendly service.
I know authentic Indian snacks. This reached that level. Fresh and baked not fried so it won’t be too heavy for a quick break. Good addition, nice staff and clean store

reviews:
I ordered panner Kathi roll and honestly it was terrible. The chapati was completely burnt , and the filling was minimal- barely any green pepper or other ingredients that’s I have enjoyed at other royal paan branches. This is second time I have received poor -quality food which is very disappointing.
Additionally I tried to order a masala dosa for $12 dollars but they didn’t have it . The burger priced at $10 , was charged $12 and was oily , greasy and taste terrible - definitely unhealthy. Overall the quality and service was below expectations and I won’ be ordering from here again

Bought two samosa chaat but I had to look deep to find samosa in it. It was all Chana (chick peas) more like I bought chickpeas curry and also it was so watery, I have had samosa Chaat from another location of Royal paan and it was good.

Absolutely disgusting. Who came up with this flavour

Absolutely shit

DO NOT BUY KESAR FLAVOUR
ITS EITHER ROTTEN OR FAKE KESAR

DOESNT TASTE GOOD
Worst food ever dont recomend anyone

I wish i could give a negative review. We ordered 2 paan and were charged overpriced over other royal paan locations. And the old guy serving us was very difficult to understand as he was busy watching tv and making items for us. Not recommending this location to anyone.

HABIBZ CORNER||356 QUEEN ST E M5A 1T1.matchedAddress: 356 Queen St E, Toronto, ON M5A 1T1, Canada
reviews: Habibz corner is a very lowkey but a very lovely restaUrant. I can’t believe i have never tried their food. Their poutine is so amazing it melts in the mouth. I also took their gorilla fries it was a burst of taste . They also offer some really tasty desserts. Their staff is also very friendly especially Assim was very polite and had a very good work ethics. I would highly recommend this restaurant.

Habibz Corner is amazing. The food and the service were very nice. I really enjoyed the chicken burger and the tandoori chicken with rice. The service provided by the girl named Yangji was excellent-she was very polite and kind. My friends also enjoyed the food.

Habibz Corner in East Toronto is truly one of my favorite spots! The food is always fresh, flavorful, and made with care — every dish tastes authentic and perfectly seasoned. The portions are generous and the prices are very reasonable for the quality you get.

A special thank you to Shiny and Rajni for their outstanding service. They are always welcoming, friendly, and attentive, making every visit feel comfortable and enjoyable. Their positive attitude and professionalism really make a difference.

If you’re in East Toronto and looking for delicious food with excellent customer service, Habi

If you’ve ever wondered what a depressed mozzarella stick looks like, wonder no more. I ordered cheese sticks and got what can only be described as breaded, floppy sadness. The cheese inside wasn’t melted — just cold, solid, and suffering with ED.

Now, to be fair — the chicken tenders here are actually solid. Crispy, flavorful, no complaints.

But these cheese sticks?
Don’t get it.
The veggie platter was absolutely delicious. The seasoning was perfect. Will definitely come back more often
HABIBZ CORNER||356 QUEEN ST E M5A 1T1.query: HABIBZ CORNER 356 QUEEN ST E Toronto

HABIBZ CORNER||2088A LAWRENCE AVE E M1R.matchedAddress: 2088A Lawrence Ave E, Scarborough, ON M1R 2Z5, Canada
reviews: Had a great meal with my sister at this location. We both had a chicken platter. Mine had fries on the side and hers had salad. The habibz white sauce is sooo good. We did visit at a later hour and had the place to ourselves for a bit . Overall we enjoyed our visit. The cashier/server Salma was definitely a big part of our pleasant visit. Thank you for your great Customer service and we hope to see you upon our next visit. Keep smiling 😊❤.

A great place to be if you are craving food in the middle of the night.
I reached Habibz close to 12am fearing they might be closing but learnt its open till 3am.
I was assisted by Ms Salma, a lively lady with a beautiful smile and she suggested me to try Montreal smoked poutine.
The food arrived with great presentation. It was my first time trying it and I liked it a lot.
I thank the chef, the team and Ms Salma for making my late night great.
Here are a The food was fresh and tasted great. Salma provided really nice, attentive service. I'd recommend this place
Great place! The food was fresh and full of flavor. Friendly staff and fast service, perfect for a quick meal. Cozy atmosphere and good prices. Definitely coming back again.

Habibiz is a new restaurant in this plaza. They are off to great start .
They have daily specials and a huge menu including dessert's thats open  late . Jay was very friendly and took my order. I got the Wednesday special of the "Gorilla fries" it did not disappoint. Freshly cut fries with seasoned beef and sauce. I will definitely be back
HABIBZ CORNER||2088A LAWRENCE AVE E M1R.query: HABIBZ CORNER 2088A LAWRENCE AVE E Toronto

reviews: Best chicken skewers in the city! Speaking as someone with severe digestive issues, this place is fantastic for good, clean food. Chicken is basically the only meat that I can eat without getting serious stomach upset, but the way that most restaurants prepare usually bothers my guts a bit. All the sauces and spices overwhelm my system, but I've never had any issues with Khayal Shawarma's meat. It's so flavorful despite the fact that it's not covered from end to end in heavy sauces. They have their marinade, but for the most part, the meat speaks for itself, and it's wonderful. The smell of th

This place is big and their menu is a lot bigger

I had the chicken shawarma platter, it comes with rice, salad or fries !!
Side of hummus and tahini

The guys working here were nice to give me all 3.

Their indoor dining was. A problem cuz their door didn’t close and it got very cold.

Coming to the food- the chicken smelled and tasted dry even with a lot of hammus and tahini.

Maybe next time try their shawrma instead !

I went to the grand opening and had mixed feelings. First I loved the chicken shawarma deal which was so delicious! It was bursting with flavours and good size too. The service at counter was friendly too.

My complaint is that I was actually second person at the grand opening but I was pissed off (along with people behind me) because the first person ordered 20 free shawarmas! We didn’t think that was fair to us! The management should have put a cap on limits per person! So we frustratedly waited a long time.

But then to add to my anger they called number 3 to pick up their order and I was p

Overall a average experience. The chicken platter I was served contained a criminal lack of chicken fractions of what you would expect from a chain competitor. My gf got a wrap at a the same time for cheaper with more chicken. Flavor was ok and chef could not understand words like pineapple drink and spicy, two things they serve.

Advice to the restaurant is to give proper or generous servings(maybe I got unlucky).

Great food, great service, the staff is always friendly. Mahmoud was the one who served me - he was friendly and patient and answered any questions I had. The food was and is always amazing, hits the spot every time! Would recommend any of their platters and shawarma!

SHAWARMA WEST||8 YORK ST, #1-3 M5J 2Y2.matchedAddress: 8 York St #1, Toronto, ON M5J 2Y2, Canada
reviews:
I just had my lunch at Shawarma West near 8th York, and it was absolutely fantastic! I ordered the beef wrap, and it was perfectly seasoned with just the right balance of flavor and freshness. What I loved most was the variety of toppings and sauces — from lettuce and olives....to their amazing signature sauces — everything was prepared exactly the way I liked it.

A special thank you to Dheeraj and Dipen for their warm service and great recommendations! They made sure I had the best combination of ingredients to make my wrap flavorful and satisfying.

If you're in downtown Toronto and craving

Loved our visit here! The food is super tasty and fresh—the shawarma plates are loaded with tender, flavorful meat, crisp salad, and soft, warm pita. The sauces really bring everything together perfectly.

The staff are super friendly and welcoming, which makes the whole experience even better. There’s plenty of seating inside, so it’s a nice spot to sit down and enjoy your meal.

And don’t skip dessert—the baklava is perfectly flaky and sweet, the best way to finish off a great meal.

Came after the Maple leafs game this past weekend, and this is exactly what I was missing from having an amazing day. It was roughly a 10 minute walk from the stadium and it was worth the wait until after the game was done cause the portion and price was just right. Really good sauces, generous amount of chicken and great staff. If you’re ever in the area I’d give them a try.

Toronto is full of shawarma places, however this is one of the good ones.

The restaurant seems very clean. The stuff wears gloves and they are very friendly. The food was ready in no time.

I ordered mixed ( chicken & beef ) personal plate. It was delicious. The portion was big.

I would definitely recommend this place for casual quick meal.
Stopped by for a quick bite. There were always long lines and the staff were friendly, though the beef was a bit on the dry and salty side for me.

HABIBZ CORNER||578 QUEEN ST W M5V 2B5.matchedAddress: 578 Queen St W, Toronto, ON M5V 2B7, Canada
reviews: Appreciate how late this establishment stays open. The double cheese burger is a 4/5. It’s tasty but the beef is well done cooked and my thoughts are it would taste better if it was cooked medium. The Montreal poutine was super delicious and really meaty and cheesy. The chai here also hits the spot and is tasty. Overall a good 4.5 Star halal burger spot.

Have eaten here a few times
Beef isn’t always fresh
Offer good deals
Need some work preparations
Quantity is decent

Honestly one of the best restaurant to get high quality and good quantity food! I ordered in uber sooo many times from this place! Food tastes good and they are so generous! I really appreciate this place!

recently visited Habibz Corner West and had a great experience. The food was delicious, fresh, and full of flavor. The service was excellent, especially from Puspa — she was very friendly, attentive, and welcoming throughout our visit. She made sure we were comfortable and satisfied with everything. I would definitely recommend this place to others and will visit again! 😊

An amazing place that's open super late! Quality food open very late at a fair price. They give 25% discount to TTC workers aswell. I got a large butterchicken poutine and it was amazing. Super filling and tasty it cost about 19$ with tax. I've had the jerk chicken too but didn't like it as much. The gorilla fries are also very good. They got daily deals aswell so highly recommend trying them out!

reviews:
Yesterday I went for lunch at Garliki, a Mediterranean restaurant that pleasantly surprised me. I loved that you can build your bowl or pita by choosing the toppings you like most—a delicious and super customizable option. The food was really tasty, with fresh ingredients and well-balanced flavors.

The place was spotlessly clean, which always inspires confidence. Plus, the service was excellent: the staff was very kind and attentive, making the whole experience even more enjoyable.
In short: great food, nice atmosphere, and excellent service. I definitely want to go back.

I choose to support local business and after my second visit I’ll never return. For 18.95$ ($1.10) tip included I was treated to one tablespoon of cauliflower, a tablespoon of salad and a portion of rice and bland dry chicken. This was the plate option.. an additional 5$ from the bowl. The extra charge is for the larger container with the same portion of food. Incredibly dissatisfied.
Great portions. Great price. Great food. Great staff. Nothing else to say. Highly recommend for all who are in the area. I come here once a week and it’s consistently a good experience.

To start off the service was great with the lady helping us helping us in picking what to get and offering us samples of the food before we got it same with there fresh juices. After order our food One chicken molasses bowl, and one steak and chicken molasses bowl. The food came out soon and both were big portions.  Overall great place to go and will definitely be back

This place will become very busy and popular very quickly! Happy to see they are finally open. Polite staff, huge portions, fresh food, so many great options. Line ups will be out the door before you know it. Will be back again soon.

SHIVA'S COFFEE BAR & SNACKS||3867 LAWREN.matchedAddress: 3867 Lawrence Ave E, Scarborough, ON M1G 1R2, Canada
reviews:
I always enjoy coming to Shiva’s Coffee Bar! Their cold coffee is consistently amazing—refreshing, creamy, and just perfect. The paninis are delicious every single time.
A special shoutout to Gopi, who serves me all the time with such kindness and great service. She’s always welcoming, friendly, and makes the experience even better.
This place has become my go-to spot, and I highly recommend it to anyone looking for great coffee, tasty food, and wonderful service! ☕🥪💛

Shiva’s is a must-visit for anyone who loves authentic Indian street food!

The food is absolutely delicious with perfect flavors and freshness in every bite. From the frankies and burgers to pav bhaji and butter maska bun, everything tastes just like India. The quality, portion size, and pricing are all on point.

This is best place which reminds me of actual our night out cafe vibes best food. Cold coco is best i never tried anywhere in Toronto. I loved their sandwich and Manchurian also. I felt satisfied after so long. Their prices are also reasonable as compare to other places. Staff is friendly. Best place to fill tummy and chilling out with friends.

Enjoyed the Chana Kulcha, Shiva's Special Italian Club, and Masala tea. Everything tasted incredible. The Italian club was vegetarian, with what I think is paneer inside. The kulcha was my favourite because it was so pillowy, light, and fluffy. I would order an extra kulcha next time.

For two people, it was around $35. Overall, it was a great spot for a light lunch. Will need to bring more family and friends here!

I’m from the USA and tried Shiva’s Coffee Bar for the first time – it was amazing! The chili garlic bread was the best I’ve ever had, full of flavor and perfectly crispy. The cold coco was super refreshing and tasted just like real Indian cold cocoa. Vadapav is amazing.I also loved the Manchurian – it was spicy, crispy, and really tasty. If you’re looking for pure Indian taste, this place is a must-visit.
SHIVA'S COFFEE BAR & SNACKS||3867 LAWREN.query: SHIVA'S COFFEE BAR & SNACKS 3867 LAWRENCE AVE E Toronto

AAHA! TRULY SOUTH DOWNTOWN||250 DUNDAS S.matchedAddress: 250 Dundas St W Suite # 101, Toronto, ON M5T 2Y8, Canada
reviews:
It's a self service restaurant and will definitely give you a vibe of a dosa place. I tried -
1. Mysore Masala dosa - crispy, evenly cooked dosa with mashed potatoes filing that will melt in your mouth
2. Vada sambhar - Freshly cooked served dipped unless you ask it for separate.
3. Tomato bajji mixture - Dry evening snack for starters. It was different
Everything was delicious. Washroom was clean, helpful staff.
The only complaint I have is the quantity of sambar served with dosa. Maybe replacing it with the 8-12oz disposable container would be enough but what's served in the plate right now

Never in my life I had Ghee Podi Idli this good. Been dreaming of it ever since. The staff was also wonderful and friendly here and I highly recommend trying this place if you’re craving authentic south Indian food. The Mysore masala dosa was also very tasty. Can’t wait to go back soon!

Amazing meals and service.
The 2 young ladies working at the counter are truly wonderful.  They make the dining experience pleasurable, are very knowledgeable about the menu. The young lady at the cash actually remembered me from the last visit and what meal we had ordered. What was amazing was she remembered my peanut allergies!! Remarkable!

Food was really good. We ordered a few different items and everything came out pretty quickly. The taste felt very close to authentic South Indian food, which I liked a lot. Service was friendly and smooth, and the ambience was nice and comfortable with soothing music. Overall, a good place to eat and I’d definitely go back.
My recommendations: badam milk, mysore bonda, punugulu, mirapakai bajji

Visited Aaha Truly South recently and absolutely loved it! We tried Punugulu, Veg Biryani, Mirchi Bajji, and Mysore Bonda — all were fresh, tasty, and delicious. The food truly felt like authentic South Indian home-style cooking. A must-try pure veg restaurant in Toronto for anyone craving real South Indian flavors. Highly recommend — you’ll definitely feel like you’re eating at home!😋
AAHA! TRULY SOUTH DOWNTOWN||250 DUNDAS S.query: AAHA! TRULY SOUTH DOWNTOWN 250 DUNDAS ST W, #102 Toronto

reviews: This restaurant used to be a 2nd location for Ghoroaa but i heard one of the partners pulled out so they had to change ownership. I had previously visited the restaurant and loved the food and ambiance so I was disappointed when I heard about the management change but after recently visiting the restaurant, I was very pleased. Now the same Khazana from Dhaka has made its debut to Toronto. The quality of the dishes is exceptional, the service is great, the plates are ceramics imported from BD, the fresh sweets are flown in straight from their Dhaka branch. It was a delicious and nice experience

I went to Khazana for dinner with my family and we all loved it! The food was super tasty the kebabs were juicy, the grilled fish was perfectly spiced, and the naan was fresh and soft. The staff were friendly, the service was quick, and the whole atmosphere felt really comfortable. We had such a good time and will definitely be coming back again!

Had a wonderful dinner at Khazana by Khazana Mithai with all my friends. The food was absolutely delicious—especially the kabab platters with naan, full of flavor and perfectly cooked. Great food, great company, and such a memorable evening.

Food is as usual good. We always eat here during weekend. Iftar items taste amazing as usual. But disappointed with the take away service. This is how they packed alur chop; 4 to 5 alu r chop was completely  mashed. Please consider giving them in a box, do not give them in paper packet all jumbled and smashed together.
they say 1 halim box for 1 person which is 13 cad. We did not check the size. We decided to take 5 for 6 people. This dish in picture is four box of their halim. It is good they give more quantity per person. Just be mindful that you might not need one box for each person. For m

The beef Bihari kabab, shish kababs  we had was so juicy and testy that it melted in my mouth.  I took a sweet box takeout and will return soon to explore their other item’s from there menus

Highly recommend for a bomb meal and would come back.

Sent my wife to pick up some food because back in the day not only was the prices good so was the food. Well things have changed not sure if management changed but this was the worse curry goat I have had in years, my wife curry chicken was pure bones no taste of curry was so bland, to add insult to injury the customer service was absent for this day.

JPPANIPURI||2687 KIPLING AVE, #10A M9V 5.matchedAddress: 2687 Kipling Ave #10A, Etobicoke, ON M9V 5G6, Canada
reviews:
We bought the puris from  here yesterday, and they were authentic, fresh, and delicious! The service was also excellent, friendly, and quick, and the prices were very decent.

Just one minor suggestion for the chef: while they were crispy, if they could be made even lighter and crispier (as opposed to harder), they would be absolutely perfect. We will definitely be back for more and highly recommend this spot!

JP Panipuri is a must-visit spot for anyone who loves authentic street food. We tried the panipuri and churmu, and both were absolutely delicious. The panipuri was perfectly crispy, filled with flavorful stuffing, and the pani had the right balance of spice and tanginess. The churmu was equally amazing—fresh, crunchy, and packed with bold flavors that kept us wanting more. Everything tasted fresh and hygienic, and the quality was impressive. The service was quick and friendly, making the experience even better. JP Panipuri truly delivers great taste at a great value. Highly recommended! 🌶️😋
I love the place and people. You can even get real indian puries. Staff is really frendly and was easygoing.

We really enjoyed our visit to JP Pani Puri! The food was fresh, flavorful, and absolutely delicious. The pani puri was perfectly prepared and tasted amazing. The staff were very friendly and welcoming, which made our experience even better. It was definitely worth the visit, and we will be coming back again! 😊

Absolutely loved it!
We tried the pani puri here and it was honestly just like the India-wali redi wali pani puri 🤍
The pani had the perfect balance of tangy, spicy, and fresh flavors, and the puris were crisp and fresh. Every bite brought back memories of street food from India.
If you’re craving authentic Indian pani puri taste, this place is a must-try. Highly recommended! 🌶️😋

ALI'S SHAWARMA||1172 WESTON RD, B M6M 4P.matchedAddress: 1172 Weston Rd, York, ON M6M 4P4, Canada
reviews: Well deserved 5 stars for this shawerma joint!, family-run business with amazing food quality, and cleanliness. The salads are obviously fresh, everything in the store is clean which indicates the hygienic practices. Definitely recommend for anyone around to try!

Popped in this afternoon and picked up a chicken shawarma wrap and chicken shawarma plate to go.  I was not disappointed!

Service was great, I was served by a lovely lady that was very pleasant.

In terms of the food itself, it was great!  It was nice and flavourful, the wrap had a nice chargrilled taste to it which I'm not as used to with shawarma but it totally worked and was absolutely delicious!

Some of my favourite shawarma places are great but I find there is always a piece of chicken I have to spit out, whether it's because there is a piece of bone, grizzle or a part that was pressed

One of the best shawarma spots!!! I’ve never been anywhere that can beat both the price and taste. In general everything was very very fairly priced. I got the fries with chicken shawarma with garlic, ranch, and honey garlic sauce and it was amazing. Fries were crispy, sauce was plentiful, and chicken shawarma was fresh, juicy, and crispy. Highly recommend. Fully Halal as well!!!
Small little shawarma shop at Weston and Eglinton. The shawarma is fresh and tasty with good size portions and the prices are good
This place is amazing with delicious food and great affordable prices with Generous portions to enjoy…… if your in the area it’s a must try and the staff are super friendly

reviews:
I almost don’t want to give away this secret, but Shawarma Style is a hidden gem! This little shawarma joint serves up an incredible wrap that’s as big as Shaquille’s shoe and priced extremely fairly. I was impressed even before taking a bite!

This is by far one of the best-tasting shawarmas I’ve ever had. It’s crispy on the outside, juicy on the inside, and the bread is incredibly flavorful. I noticed they had fresh beef cooking, which is a rarity! Most places serve cold beef that’s just warmed up.

The owner is such a gentleman and a humble host; you can really feel his warm, kind energy. I
Great Saj Chicken Shawarma- will be coming back!  Huge portion, nicely grilled, chicken was perfectly cooked and even had the pomegranate sauce. Friendly staff to boot

101 reasons to visit shawarma style! Really delicious, services is so friendly and portions are great! So kind, even received some extra protein on the side for our buddy here. Wonderful addition to the neighbourhood ❤️

I'll be visiting this place quite often! The shawarma (Saj wrap) is quite generous, the portion of chicken is better than other shawarma places close by.  Also, the veggies are fresh and the sauces tasty.  Recommended!

The food here is amazing

always fresh and warm- everything is made to order and is quality

my go-to is the saj wrap with fries the fries are a larger portion for sure always so full and satisfied after eating here

This is definitely a new gem in our neighborhood!

EL SABOR DEL PACIFICO RESTAURANT||2111 J.matchedAddress: 2111 Jane St unit 7, North York, ON M3M 1A2, Canada
reviews:
This used to be a great spot for Colombian food, but unfortunately the service has gone downhill. Unless the owner is present, the waitstaff can be quite unfriendly. On my last visit, the food arrived overcooked and burned. Really disappointing, as this place used to be one of my favorites.
Don’t waste your money.
If you what to taste the colombian coast food, here is the place. we love the food and the service; Luis the owner is really kind and always brings us the best…

Exquisite soups, and typical Colombian seafood dishes. I have also tried the oxtail soup and it is delicious, they have different methods of payment and now luckily for many they are also on several food apps, I recommend viewing the menu through one of them and then just ordering via phone, I always do take out. Our home favorite is the cazuela de mariscos(seafood chowder) ...try it, it's even better than many I have tried when traveling to Colombia.

Look at the pics below,
they are $30 badeja paisa and $40 fried tilapia(no picture),and the staffs have disgusting attitude.if I visit any of my latino friend I would be treated x10,000 better.
trust me.this place will make you repent that you spend your gas to drive to.you can thank me.
Amazing food the Pacific of Colombia! Highly recommend the sancocho! The staff were friendly and I was able to get takeout. The food traveled well despite being a soup!
